message:
Here's a fun one that explains a lot: your cat literally cannot taste sweetness. 🐱🍰

Somewhere far back in their evolution — as cats became pure meat-eaters — the gene that builds the "sweet" taste receptor quietly switched off and never came back. Scientists have confirmed it: a cat's tongue has no working detector for sugar at all. So that frosting or ice cream they come over to sniff? They're curious about the fat or the smell, not the sweetness — to them, sugar tastes like nothing.

It's a tidy little reminder that cats are carnivores right down to their taste buds. 🍗

Comment 🐾 if your cat begs for a bite of your food but couldn't care less about anything sweet!
